redemption_fee.note: Most banks charge 99 + GST per reward redemption event — factor this in before redeeming small amounts

dcc_fee_percent.note: Extra 1% when you pay in INR abroad instead of local currency. Always pay in local currency when travelling.

rent_payment_fee_percent.note: Most banks charge 1% processing fee on rent payments via credit card. No reward points earned on rent.

education_payment_fee_percent.note: 1% fee on school and college fee payments. Check if rewards are earned before using card.

fuel_surcharge.waiver_condition: Most cards waive fuel surcharge between 400 to 5000 per transaction
fuel_surcharge.note: Always use a card with fuel surcharge waiver at petrol pumps

cash_advance_fee.note: Never use credit card for cash withdrawal — 2.5% fee plus interest from day one with no grace period

emi_processing_fee.note: Banks charge 1-2% processing fee to convert purchases to EMI. No-cost EMI is paid by merchant, not you.

over_limit_fee.note: Charged if spending exceeds credit limit. Avoid by setting spend alerts.

late_payment_penalties.credit_score_impact: Late payment reported to CIBIL after 30 days overdue. Severely damages credit score.
late_payment_penalties.advice: Always set auto-pay for at least minimum due. Never miss a payment.

interest_rates.critical_warning: Credit card interest is the most expensive debt in India at 42-49% per year. Never carry a balance. Always pay full outstanding every month.
interest_rates.minimum_due_trap: Paying only minimum due means you pay 42-49% interest on remaining balance. On 10,000 balance you pay 350-400 per month just in interest.

reward_rules.expiry_warning: Set calendar reminder 3 months before expiry to redeem points
devaluations_2025_2026: HDFC Infinia — redemption capped 5 times per month from February 2026
HDFC Infinia — insurance points capped at 10,000 per month
HDFC Diners Black — insurance points capped at 5,000 per month
Axis Magnus — utility rewards capped at 500 per month from 2025
Axis Magnus — insurance rewards capped at 2,000 per month
Axis ACE — 5% category capped at 500 per month from 2025
ICICI Sapphiro — movie benefits discontinued February 2026
SBI Elite and Prime — air accident insurance discontinued July 2025
IndusInd EazyDiner — dining multiplier cut from 5X to 1.5X June 2025
IDFC FIRST Wealth — lounge now requires 20,000 monthly spend
Tata Neu — lounge now requires 50,000 quarterly spend
ICICI Sapphiro — lounge now requires 75,000 quarterly spend

lounge_access_rules.insight: 45% of cardholders with lounge access never use it — check your card and plan visits before benefit expires

spend_unlock_range.note: Most cards now require previous month or quarter spend to unlock lounge access

card_specific_requirements.HDFC_Regalia_Gold: 1 lakh quarterly spend to unlock lounge

lounge_access_rules.guest_charges: Most lounges charge 500-1000 per guest entry if not covered by card
lounge_access_rules.priority_pass_tip: Priority Pass covers 1400+ lounges globally. Cards like Infinia, Magnus, Emeralde include it.

forex_rules.worst_cards_for_forex: SBI, Kotak, Axis ACE — 3.5% standard markup
forex_rules.saving_calculation: On 1 lakh international spend, zero forex card saves 3,500 vs standard card
forex_rules.tip: Always carry a zero or low forex markup card for international travel

credit_score_rules.hard_inquiry_impact: Each new card application creates hard inquiry — reduces score by 5-10 points temporarily
credit_score_rules.payment_history_weight: 35% of credit score — most important factor

score_ranges.300_549: Poor — very hard to get loans

tips: Pay full outstanding every month — not just minimum
Do not apply for multiple cards in same month
Keep old cards active — account age matters
Check CIBIL score free once a year at cibil.com

nry_formula.name: Net Reward Yield
nry_formula.formula: NRY = (Sum of CategorySpend x RewardRate) - AnnualFee / TotalAnnualSpend
nry_formula.description: Use this to compare which card gives best actual return on your specific spending pattern

emergency_fund.where_to_keep: Liquid mutual fund or high interest savings account — NOT in FD (premature penalty) or equity (volatile)
emergency_fund.how_to_build: Save 1 month expenses every 3 months until you reach 6 months
best_options: High interest savings account (IndusInd, YES Bank)

allocation_limits.note: These are hard limits — breaching these increases concentration risk significantly

best_instruments: ELSS Mutual Fund (3 year lock-in, equity returns)
5-year tax saver FD (5 year lock-in, FD returns)
section_80C.recommendation: Use ELSS for 80C if you are below 50 years. Use PPF if you want guaranteed returns.

section_80CCD_1B.note: This is OVER AND ABOVE 80C limit — additional 50,000 deduction. Saves 15,000 for 30% tax bracket.

section_80D.recommendation: Always buy health insurance — serves dual purpose of protection and tax saving

ltcg_rules.equity_ltcg: 12.5% on gains above 1.25 lakh per year — after 1 year holding
ltcg_rules.debt_ltcg: As per income slab — after April 2023 change
ltcg_rules.real_estate_ltcg: 12.5% without indexation — after 2 years (post July 2024 budget)
ltcg_rules.tip: Harvest LTCG of 1.25 lakh every year tax-free by selling and rebuying — saves significant tax over long term

sip_tips: Start as early as possible — time is the biggest factor
Increase SIP by 10% every year (step-up SIP)
Never stop SIP during market crash — this is when you buy cheap
Use direct plans — saves 0.5-1% annually vs regular plans
SIP date does not matter much — pick any date you remember
sip_rules.step_up_sip_power: 10,000 per month SIP with 10% annual step-up for 20 years at 12% = 1.86 crore vs 99.91 lakh without step-up

20_30.rationale: Maximum time horizon allows maximum equity. Volatility is your friend — you have 30+ years to recover from any crash.
priority_actions: Build 6 month emergency fund first
Max 80C via ELSS
Start Nifty 50 SIP
Open NPS account for additional 50,000 deduction

30_40.rationale: Still high equity allocation. Beginning to add stability. Home loan EMI counts as forced debt investment.
priority_actions: Max PPF and NPS contributions
Increase SIP with each salary hike
Buy term insurance if not done
Buy health insurance for family

40_50.rationale: Approaching peak earning years. Shift toward stability without abandoning growth. Retirement 15-20 years away.
priority_actions: Retirement corpus should be growing aggressively
Reduce or eliminate home loan by 45
Increase NPS allocation for tax saving
Start planning for children education goals

50_60.rationale: Approaching retirement. Capital preservation becomes important. Shift toward income generating assets.
priority_actions: Move to large cap only equity — reduce volatility
Invest in SCSS for guaranteed 8.2% post retirement
Ensure health insurance is adequate — higher cover
Start systematic withdrawal planning for retirement

above_60.rationale: Capital preservation and regular income is priority. Equity only for inflation protection on a small portion.
priority_actions: Invest 15 lakh max in SCSS at 8.2% per account
Open SCSS in spouse name also for additional 15 lakh
Post Office MIS for monthly income
Keep 2-3 year expenses in liquid fund as buffer

low.description: Capital safety over returns. Cannot tolerate seeing portfolio value fall even temporarily.
low.typical_investor: Retired individuals, very conservative savers, people with low income stability

moderate.description: Balanced approach. Willing to accept short term volatility for long term gains. 5+ year horizon.
moderate.typical_investor: Salaried professionals, first-time equity investors, investors with 5-10 year goals

high.description: Growth focused. Comfortable with 30-40% portfolio drawdowns. Investing for 7+ years.
high.typical_investor: Young professionals, business owners, investors with 10+ year horizon

very_high.description: Maximum growth orientation. Can handle 50%+ drawdowns. Long 10+ year horizon. Sophisticated investor.
suitable_instruments: All equity categories including small cap
very_high.typical_investor: High income earners, HNI investors, investors with multiple income sources, entrepreneurs

portfolio_rebalancing.when_to_rebalance: When any asset class drifts more than 5% from target allocation
how_to_rebalance: Option 1 — Redirect new SIPs to underweight asset class (simplest, no tax)
Option 2 — Sell overweight asset and buy underweight (may trigger capital gains)
Option 3 — Combination of both — partial sell plus redirect SIPs

rebalancing_example.action: Reduce equity SIP temporarily, increase debt and gold SIP until back to target

portfolio_rebalancing.tax_efficient_rebalancing: Prefer redirecting SIPs over selling to avoid capital gains tax. Only sell if drift is very large (above 10%).

savings_rate.formula: (Monthly Income - Monthly Expenses) / Monthly Income x 100

emergency_fund.note: Count months of EXPENSES not income

debt_to_income_ratio.formula: Total Monthly Debt Payments / Gross Monthly Income x 100
debt_to_income_ratio.note: Include home loan EMI, car loan EMI, personal loan EMI, credit card minimum due

india_adapted_rule.note: In India where cost of living is lower relative to income for middle class, target 30% savings over 20%

monthly_budget_priorities.action: Emergency fund contribution until 6 months reached
Term insurance and health insurance premiums
PPF contribution — max 1.5 lakh per year
Index fund SIP for long term wealth
Everything else — mid cap, international, gold ETF etc

common_financial_mistakes.mistake: Buying ULIP or endowment plan thinking it is investment
common_financial_mistakes.impact: Returns 5-7% vs 11-12% from mutual funds — costs lakhs over 20 years
common_financial_mistakes.fix: Buy term insurance separately. Invest in mutual funds.

common_financial_mistakes.mistake: Keeping emergency fund in savings account
common_financial_mistakes.impact: Earns only 3.5% vs 6.5-7% in liquid fund
common_financial_mistakes.fix: Move emergency fund to liquid mutual fund

common_financial_mistakes.mistake: Investing in regular mutual fund plans via distributor
common_financial_mistakes.impact: Pays 1-1.5% extra expense ratio annually — loses 30-40% of wealth over 20 years
common_financial_mistakes.fix: Switch to direct plans via Zerodha Coin, Groww or MF Central

common_financial_mistakes.mistake: Paying only minimum due on credit card
common_financial_mistakes.impact: Pays 42-49% annual interest on remaining balance
common_financial_mistakes.fix: Always pay full outstanding every month

common_financial_mistakes.mistake: Not buying term insurance when young
common_financial_mistakes.impact: Premium doubles every 5-7 years of delay
common_financial_mistakes.fix: Buy term insurance by age 30 — 1 crore cover costs 8,000-12,000 per year at 28

common_financial_mistakes.impact: Misses buying at cheapest prices — loses compounding benefit
common_financial_mistakes.fix: Continue SIP regardless of market level. Crash is a sale on stocks.

common_financial_mistakes.mistake: Not making a nomination in investments
common_financial_mistakes.impact: Family faces legal hurdles to claim money after death
common_financial_mistakes.fix: Add nominee in every bank account, mutual fund, insurance policy

common_financial_mistakes.impact: Forces selling investments at loss during emergencies
common_financial_mistakes.fix: Build 6 month emergency fund before investing
